<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<mule xmlns:tracking="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/tracking" xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core" xmlns:apikit="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/mule-apikit" xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation" xmlns:ee="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core" xmlns:error-handler-plugin="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/error-handler-plugin" xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http" xmlns:json-logger="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/json-logger" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsi:schemaLocation="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/mule-apikit http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/mule-apikit/current/mule-apikit.xsd http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core/current/mule-ee.xsd  http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/error-handler-plugin http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/error-handler-plugin/current/mule-error-handler-plugin.xsd http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/json-logger http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/json-logger/current/mule-json-logger.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/tracking http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/tracking/current/mule-tracking-ee.xsd">
    
    <flow name="mule-intrado-sapi-main">
        <http:listener config-ref="HTTPS_Listener_config" path="${api.name}/api/${api.version}/*">
            <http:response statusCode="#[vars.httpStatus default 200]">
                <http:headers><![CDATA[#[vars.outboundHeaders default {}]]]></http:headers>
            </http:response>
            <http:error-response statusCode="#[vars.httpStatus default 500]">
                <http:body><![CDATA[#[payload]]]></http:body>
                <http:headers><![CDATA[#[vars.outboundHeaders default {}]]]></http:headers>
            </http:error-response>
        </http:listener>
        <ee:transform doc:name="Set Transaction Id" doc:id="13e2cab4-b74a-4e9e-8177-8888fc295e32" >
			<ee:message >
			</ee:message>
			<ee:variables >
				<ee:set-variable variableName="transactionId" ><![CDATA[attributes.headers.transactionId default correlationId]]></ee:set-variable>
			</ee:variables>
		</ee:transform>
        <apikit:router config-ref="mule-intrado-sapi-config" />
        <error-handler>
            <on-error-propagate enableNotifications="true" logException="true" doc:name="On Error Propagate" doc:id="e27d03ab-66dc-4b3c-b7a1-3384ec3b0d23">
				<set-variable value='#[error.muleMessage.typedAttributes."statusCode" default 500]' doc:name="Set HTTP Status Code" doc:id="733ad535-23f2-4d00-94e6-7fb3907bdd18" variableName="httpStatus" />
				<json-logger:logger doc:name="log sttaus code" doc:id="4a983d02-8f86-491f-8b8a-bc0cd2bd988f" config-ref="JSON_Logger_Config" message="#['log status code form intrado : ' ++ vars.httpStatus]" tracePoint="EXCEPTION" correlationId="#[vars.transactionId]" />
				<error-handler-plugin:on-error doc:name="Process Error" doc:id="2a7f57d8-f725-4073-978d-fa43d1e04b48" config-ref="Error_Handler_Plugin_Config" previousErrors="#[[]]" correlationId="#[vars.transactionId]"/>
				<ee:transform doc:name="Transform Message" doc:id="a4a6b6b3-4495-4429-a2a1-45e7323a05e8">
                    <ee:message>
                        <ee:set-payload><![CDATA[%dw 2.0
output application/json
---
payload update {
	case .errorDetails -> ($) map (val, ind) -> {
		(val ++ {
			"description": if ( !isEmpty(error.muleMessage.typedValue.errors) ) error.muleMessage.typedValue.errors 
            else if ( !isEmpty(error.muleMessage.typedValue.message) ) error.muleMessage.typedValue.message
            else if ( !isEmpty(error.muleMessage.typedValue) ) error.muleMessage.typedValue 
            else [val.message],
			"system": if ( !isEmpty(error.muleMessage.typedValue) ) "Intrado" else "MuleSoft"
		})
	}
}]]></ee:set-payload>
                    </ee:message>
                    <ee:variables />
                </ee:transform>
				<json-logger:logger doc:name="logError" doc:id="d8773439-d5f3-48f2-8607-a9f52584af91" config-ref="JSON_Logger_Config" message="#['After Receiving Error']" tracePoint="EXCEPTION" correlationId="#[vars.transactionId]"/>
            </on-error-propagate>
        </error-handler>
    </flow>
    <flow name="mule-intrado-sapi-console">
        <http:listener config-ref="HTTPS_Listener_config" path="${api.name}/api/${api.version}/console/*">
            <http:response statusCode="#[vars.httpStatus default 200]">
                <http:headers><![CDATA[#[vars.outboundHeaders default {}]]]></http:headers>
            </http:response>
            <http:error-response statusCode="#[vars.httpStatus default 500]">
                <http:body><![CDATA[#[payload]]]></http:body>
                <http:headers><![CDATA[#[vars.outboundHeaders default {}]]]></http:headers>
            </http:error-response>
        </http:listener>
        <apikit:console config-ref="mule-intrado-sapi-config" />
        <error-handler>
            <on-error-propagate enableNotifications="true" logException="true" doc:name="On Error Propagate1" doc:id="b840b81a-69f3-4ec0-a2fd-a78975f7bde0">
                <error-handler-plugin:on-error doc:name="Process Error" doc:id="0030941c-49c1-4a0e-9195-cd57a9df4ea9" config-ref="Error_Handler_Plugin_Config" previousErrors="#[flatten([error.errorMessage.payload default []])]" />
                <set-variable value="#[attributes.httpStatus]" doc:name="Set HTTP Status Code" doc:id="71ba0eb7-3cae-4f00-bba8-762a74ca926c" variableName="httpStatus" />
                <json-logger:logger doc:name="logError" doc:id="13f01779-dc7f-4e9f-a6a8-d047e0b65882" config-ref="JSON_Logger_Config" message="#['After Receiving Error']" tracePoint="EXCEPTION" correlationId="#[vars.transactionId]"/>
            </on-error-propagate>
        </error-handler>
    </flow>
    <flow name="post:\pinxt\request:application\xml:mule-intrado-sapi-config">
        <json-logger:logger doc:name="Start" doc:id="071ba677-febf-4d3c-8547-ce3e4b0e85e7" config-ref="JSON_Logger_Config" message="#["intrado request flow started"]" correlationId="#[vars.transactionId]"/>
        <flow-ref doc:name="intrado-request-subflow" doc:id="714b9f06-ce09-4a74-91b1-36a8d3bc59ce" name="intrado-request-subflow" />
        <json-logger:logger doc:name="End" doc:id="d42a9376-3afb-4281-9152-a2522d034aba" config-ref="JSON_Logger_Config" message="#["intrado request flow ended"]" tracePoint="END" correlationId="#[vars.transactionId]"/>
    </flow>
    <flow name="get:\pinxt\healthcheck:mule-intrado-sapi-config">
        <json-logger:logger doc:name="Start" doc:id="6c52eec2-4b7b-40dc-b978-b9880e3709b3" config-ref="JSON_Logger_Config" message='#["intrado healthcheck request flow started"]' correlationId="#[vars.transactionId]"/>
		<ee:transform>
            <ee:message>
                <ee:set-payload><![CDATA[%dw 2.0
output application/json
---
{
	success: true,
	"apiName": Mule::p('api.name'),
	"version": Mule::p('api.version'),
	"correlationId": correlationId,
	timestamp: now(),
	response: {
		status: 200,
		message: "Ok!"
	}
}]]></ee:set-payload>
            </ee:message>
        </ee:transform>
		<json-logger:logger doc:name="End" doc:id="28c79a19-de02-4dad-a764-2806c32df54c" config-ref="JSON_Logger_Config" message='#["intrado healthcheck request flow ended"]' tracePoint="END" correlationId="#[vars.transactionId]"/>
    </flow>
</mule>

The **SF SPC PAPI** (Process API) serves as the integration layer between Salesforce and the Spectrum Platform Core (SPC) system. It provides a unifie using DataWeave Transform, HTTP Request, Scatter-Gather (parallel)
output:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

<mule xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http" xmlns:ee="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core"
	xmlns:json-logger="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/json-logger"
	xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core" xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsi:schemaLocation="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/json-logger http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/json-logger/current/mule-json-logger.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core/current/mule-ee.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd">
	<flow name="post-accountFinancials-flow" doc:id="96b9d2c3-de82-4324-9f53-3450a8addf5a" >
		<json-logger:logger doc:name="InitialLog" doc:id="d1e1cd18-4cea-4c03-8fda-f8ff30225514" config-ref="JSON_Logger_Config" message="getAccountFinancials :: Intial Payload :"/>
		<scatter-gather doc:name="Scatter-Gather" doc:id="92fca5ed-8399-4dc3-94bf-947a9879b551" >
			<route >
				<ee:transform doc:name="RequestPayload" doc:id="dc9aab87-eb26-40e3-9607-ccd3b368e1e1" >
					<ee:message >
						<ee:set-payload resource="dw/SPC_getAccount_Request.dwl" />
					</ee:message>
				</ee:transform>
				<json-logger:logger doc:name="RequestLog" doc:id="bf33561c-9b16-43f5-b161-157978684710" config-ref="JSON_Logger_Config" message=" getAccountFinancials :: SPC Service : addAccountV1x1 :: Request :"/>
				<http:request method="POST" doc:name="SPC : getAccountV1x1" doc:id="42bc87d4-5fb6-45a0-a00e-fb6da3dec835" config-ref="SPC_HTTPS_Request_configuration" path="${spc.getAccountV1x1.InvokePath}" sendCorrelationId="ALWAYS" correlationId="#[correlationId]" />
				<json-logger:logger doc:name="ResponseLog" doc:id="640cda49-4986-4679-92ef-7f65652ab919" config-ref="JSON_Logger_Config" message="getAccountFinancials :: SPC Service : addAccountV1x1 :: Response :"/>
			</route>
			<route >
				<ee:transform doc:name="RequestPayload" doc:id="91e228d6-4544-406a-91f8-3b6ad626ece9">
					<ee:message>
						<ee:set-payload resource="dw/SPC_getAccountFinancials_getCustomerStatements_Request.dwl" />
					</ee:message>
				</ee:transform>
				<json-logger:logger doc:name="RequestLog" doc:id="8035adfc-3988-4af7-bf2e-214b62204abe" config-ref="JSON_Logger_Config" message="getAccountFinancials :: SPC Service : getCustomerStatementsV1x1 :: Request :"/>
				<http:request method="POST" doc:name="SPC : getCustomerStatementsV1x1" doc:id="9932bca3-8218-4e21-b998-927ae4f945da" path="${spc.getCustomerStatementsV1x1.InvokePath}" config-ref="SPC_HTTPS_Request_configuration" sendCorrelationId="ALWAYS" correlationId="#[correlationId]" />
				<json-logger:logger doc:name="ResponseLog" doc:id="37fa41bc-ee87-445f-a3a6-179dc3b9f648" config-ref="JSON_Logger_Config" message="getAccountFinancials :: SPC Service : getCustomerStatementsV1x1 :: Response :"/>
			</route>
		</scatter-gather>
		<ee:transform doc:name="Transform SPC Response" doc:id="b1d7e304-4fa8-4a6e-94dd-df6fa4e05369">
					<ee:message>
				<ee:set-payload resource="dw/SPC_accountFinancials_Response.dwl" />
					</ee:message>
				</ee:transform>
		<json-logger:logger doc:name="SPC Final Response Log" doc:id="6fc58695-6b25-4391-a62d-19ea0de72980" config-ref="JSON_Logger_Config" message=" getAccountFinancials ::  Successfully Processed : Response :"/>
			<error-handler >
			<on-error-continue enableNotifications="true" logException="true" doc:name="On Error Continue" doc:id="705a35dd-a7f5-410a-b94d-f4cd8c312164" >
				<set-variable value="#[error.errors[0].exception.errorMessage.attributes.statusCode]" doc:name="httpStatus" doc:id="9b1d0736-e7b9-408c-bb0f-f31dc21a6d4f" variableName="httpStatus" />
				<ee:transform doc:name="Transform Error Message" doc:id="1abc45b1-006c-4a92-9e50-d2e60a4e8300">
					<ee:message>
						<ee:set-payload resource="dw/SPC_accountFinancials_Error_Response.dwl" />
					</ee:message>
				</ee:transform>
				<json-logger:logger doc:name="SPC Error Log" doc:id="afbefacc-d8ee-4a7a-83f5-08dfbcd1b112" config-ref="JSON_Logger_Config" tracePoint="EXCEPTION" message="SPC Service :  getAccountFinancials :: Final Exception Message : " />
			</on-error-continue>
		</error-handler>
	</flow>
</mule>

instruction: Write a DataWeave transformation for v o2fileRecords (mapping/transformation)
output:
%dw 2.0
output application/java
---
// Sort the payload by "Task Creation Date" in descending order to prioritize the latest records
// Then remove duplicates based on "Unique ID", keeping only the first occurrence (which will be the latest due to the sort)

(payload orderBy ((item, index) -> item."Task Creation Date"))[-1 to 0] distinctBy ((item, index) -> item."Unique ID" ) map {
	"accountNumber" : $.'Account Number',
	"Work_Order_Number__c" : $.'Work Order Number',
	"Source__c" : $.'Created By-Dep',
	"Name" : $.'Unique ID',
	"Type" : $.'Primary Disposition',
	"Sub_Type__c" : $.'Secondary Disposition',
	"Detail__c" : $.'Third Level Disposition',
	"External_Created_Date__c" : $.'Task Creation Date',
	"ActivityDate" : $.'Follow-up Till Date',
	"Status" : $.'Task Status',
	"Comment__c" : $.'Comments'
}

instruction: Write a DataWeave transformation for shakarbisetty mulesoft cookbook canonical data model (mapping/transformation, aggregation)
output:
/**
 * Pattern: Canonical Data Model
 * Category: Real-World Mappings
 * Difficulty: Advanced
 * Description: Normalize data from multiple source systems into a single
 * canonical (standard) format. The canonical data model pattern reduces
 * point-to-point mappings from N*M to N+M by establishing a common
 * intermediate format. Each source maps TO canonical, each target maps
 * FROM canonical.
 *
 * Input (application/json):
 * {
 *   "source": "salesforce",
 *   "salesforceRecord": {
 *     "Id": "001A000001abc",
 *     "Name": "Acme Corp",
 *     "BillingStreet": "100 Main St",
 *     "BillingCity": "Austin",
 *     "BillingState": "TX",
 *     "BillingPostalCode": "78701",
 *     "Phone": "512-555-0100",
 *     "Website": "https://acme.com",
 *     "Industry": "Technology",
 *     "CreatedDate": "2025-03-15T10:30:00Z"
 *   },
 *   "sapRecord": {
 *     "Id": "SAP-100",
 *     "Name": "SAP Fallback",
 *     "BillingStreet": "N/A",
 *     "BillingCity": "N/A",
 *     "BillingState": "N/A",
 *     "BillingPostalCode": "00000",
 *     "Phone": "000-000-0000",
 *     "Website": "",
 *     "Industry": "Unknown",
 *     "CreatedDate": "2025-01-01T00:00:00Z"
 *   },
 *   "legacyRecord": {
 *     "Id": "LEG-200",
 *     "Name": "Legacy Fallback",
 *     "BillingStreet": "N/A",
 *     "BillingCity": "N/A",
 *     "BillingState": "N/A",
 *     "BillingPostalCode": "00000",
 *     "Phone": "000-000-0000",
 *     "Website": "",
 *     "Industry": "Unknown",
 *     "CreatedDate": "2025-01-01T00:00:00Z"
 *   }
 * }
 *
 * Output (application/json):
 * {
 * "canonical": {
 * "entity": "Organization",
 * "version": "1.0",
 * "id": {
 * "canonical": "ORG-salesforce-001Dn00000XYZ789",
 * "sourceSystem": "salesforce",
 * "sourceId": "001Dn00000XYZ789"
 * },
 * "name": "Acme Corporation",
 * "address": {
 * "street": "123 Innovation Drive",
 * "city": "San Francisco",
 * "state": "CA",
 * "postalCode": "94102",
 * "country": "US"
 * },
 * "phone": "+1-555-0100",
 * "website": "https://acme.com",
 * "industry": "Technology",
 * "createdAt": "2025-03-10T08:00:00Z",
 * "updatedAt": "2026-02-14T16:45:00Z"
 * }
 * }
 */
%dw 2.0
output application/json
fun fromSalesforce(rec) = {id: rec.Id, name: rec.Name, address: {street: rec.BillingStreet, city: rec.BillingCity, state: rec.BillingState, zip: rec.BillingPostalCode}, phone: rec.Phone, website: rec.Website, industry: rec.Industry, sourceSystem: "salesforce", mappedAt: now() as String {format: "yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss'Z'"}}
---
payload.source match {"salesforce" -> fromSalesforce(payload.salesforceRecord), "sap" -> fromSalesforce(payload.sapRecord), else -> fromSalesforce(payload.legacyRecord)}

instruction: Write a DataWeave transformation: GetV3 Success
output:
%dw 2.0
output application/json
---
if (sizeOf(vars.classicVar)>0 and sizeOf(vars.lightVar)>0) {
	"success": true,
    "apiName": p('api.name'),
    "version": vars.version,
    "correlationId": correlationId,
    "timestamp": now(),
    "responseList": (vars.classicVar) ++ (vars.lightVar)
}
else 
if (sizeOf(vars.classicvar )> 0 and sizeOf(vars.lightvar) == 0)
	{ "success": true,
    "apiName": p('api.name'),
    "version": vars.version,
    "correlationId": correlationId,
    "timestamp": now(),
    "responseList": vars.classicvar }
else 
if (sizeOf(vars.lightvar )> 0 and sizeOf(vars.classicvar) == 0)
	{ "success": true,
    "apiName": p('api.name'),
    "version": vars.version,
    "correlationId": correlationId,
    "timestamp": now(),
    "responseList": vars.lightvar }
else
{
    "success": false,
    "apiName": p('api.name'),
    "version": vars.version,
    "correlationId": correlationId,
    "timestamp": now(),
    "errorDetails": [
        {
            "code": p('error.badrequestcode'),
            "reason": p('error.sfreason'),
            "message": p('error.invaliddata')
        }
    ]
}

instruction: Create a Mule 4 flow named 'orders-api-flow' using Choice Router (conditional), DataWeave Transform, Error Handling, HTTP Listener, HTTP Request with API endpoint /api/v1/orders
output:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<mule xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core"
      xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation"
      xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http"
      xmlns:ee="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core"
      xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
      xsi:schemaLocation="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
                          http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd
                          http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core/current/mule-ee.xsd">

    <!-- Well-structured HTTP configuration with timeout -->
    <http:request-config name="HTTP_Request_Configuration" 
                         doc:name="HTTP Request Configuration"
                         responseTimeout="30000">
        <http:request-connection host="${api.host}" port="${api.port}" />
    </http:request-config>

    <!-- Global Error Handler - Best Practice -->
    <error-handler name="global-error-handler" doc:name="Global Error Handler">
        <on-error-propagate type="HTTP:CONNECTIVITY" doc:name="On Error Propagate">
            <set-variable variableName="httpStatus" value="503" doc:name="Set HTTP Status"/>
            <set-variable variableName="correlationId" value="#[correlationId]" doc:name="Set Correlation ID"/>
            <logger level="ERROR" 
                    category="com.myorg.error" 
                    message='#[%dw 2.0 output application/json --- {"error": error.description, "correlationId": vars.correlationId}]'
                    doc:name="Log Error"/>
        </on-error-propagate>
        <on-error-propagate type="APIKIT:BAD_REQUEST" doc:name="On Error Propagate">
            <set-variable variableName="httpStatus" value="400" doc:name="Set HTTP Status"/>
            <set-variable variableName="correlationId" value="#[correlationId]" doc:name="Set Correlation ID"/>
            <logger level="WARN" 
                    category="com.myorg.error" 
                    message='#[%dw 2.0 output application/json --- {"error": "Bad Request", "correlationId": vars.correlationId}]'
                    doc:name="Log Warning"/>
        </on-error-propagate>
    </error-handler>

    <!-- Main API flow with proper naming and error handling -->
    <flow name="orders-api-flow" doc:name="Orders API Flow" doc:description="Main entry point for Orders API">
        <http:listener config-ref="HTTP_Listener_config" path="/api/v1/orders" doc:name="HTTP Listener"/>
        
        <logger level="INFO" 
                category="com.myorg.orders" 
                message='#[%dw 2.0 output application/json --- {"message": "Received order request", "orderId": attributes.queryParams.orderId}]'
                doc:name="Log Request"/>
        
        <flow-ref name="validate-order-subflow" doc:name="Validate Order"/>
        <flow-ref name="process-order-subflow" doc:name="Process Order"/>
        
        <ee:transform doc:name="Build Response">
            <ee:message>
                <ee:set-payload><![CDATA[%dw 2.0
output application/json
---
{
    status: "success",
    orderId: vars.orderId,
    correlationId: correlationId
}]]></ee:set-payload>
            </ee:message>
        </ee:transform>
        
        <error-handler ref="global-error-handler"/>
    </flow>

    <!-- Sub-flow with proper naming -->
    <sub-flow name="validate-order-subflow" doc:name="Validate Order Subflow" doc:description="Validates incoming order data">
        <set-variable variableName="orderId" value="#[attributes.queryParams.orderId]" doc:name="Extract Order ID"/>
        
        <choice doc:name="Check Order Type">
            <when expression="#[vars.orderId != null]">
                <logger level="DEBUG" category="com.myorg.validation" message="Order ID is valid" doc:name="Log Valid"/>
            </when>
            <otherwise>
                <raise-error type="APP:INVALID_ORDER" description="Missing order ID" doc:name="Raise Error"/>
            </otherwise>
        </choice>
    </sub-flow>

    <!-- Processing sub-flow -->
    <sub-flow name="process-order-subflow" doc:name="Process Order Subflow" doc:description="Processes the validated order">
        <http:request method="POST" config-ref="HTTP_Request_Configuration" path="/orders" doc:name="Create Order">
            <http:headers><![CDATA[#[{
                "Content-Type": "application/json",
                "User-Agent": "OrdersAPI/1.0",
                "X-Correlation-ID": correlationId
            }]]]></http:headers>
        </http:request>
        
        <logger level="INFO" 
                category="com.myorg.orders" 
                message='#[%dw 2.0 output application/json --- {"message": "Order processed", "orderId": vars.orderId}]'
                doc:name="Log Success"/>
    </sub-flow>

</mule>

instruction: Write a DataWeave transformation for multiGetServiceStatus Transform SPC Responses (mapping/transformation, filtering)
output:
%dw 2.0
output application/json
var accountNumberSize = if(payload[0].payload.getAccountResponse.account.accountNumber != null)sizeOf(payload[0].payload.getAccountResponse.account.accountNumber) else null
---
Account: {
		AccountNumber: payload[0].payload.getAccountResponse.account.accountNumber,
		AccountActiveIndicator: if(payload[0].payload.getAccountResponse.account.accountStatus == "Active")'Y' else if (payload[0].payload.getAccountResponse.account.accountStatus == "Inactive")"N" else 'None',
		AccountConnectDate: payload[0].payload.getAccountResponse.account.connectDate,
		AccountDisconnectDate: payload[0].payload.getAccountResponse.account.disconnectDate,
		IBSOrganization1: if (accountNumberSize == 16)payload[0].payload.getAccountResponse.account.billingStationLevel1Code else payload[0].payload.getAccountResponse.account.billingStationLevel0Code,
		IBSOrganization2: if (accountNumberSize == 16)payload[0].payload.getAccountResponse.account.billingStationLevel2Code else payload[0].payload.getAccountResponse.account.billingStationLevel1Code,
		IBSOrganization3: if (accountNumberSize == 16)payload[0].payload.getAccountResponse.account.sourceFTACode else payload[0].payload.getAccountResponse.account.billingStationLevel2Code,
		IBSOrganization4: payload[0].payload.getAccountResponse.account.sourceFTACode,
			CurrentService : 	((payload[1].payload.getCurrentServicesResponse.services default []) map {
				StatementCode: $.statementCode,
				IBSServiceCode: $.itemCode.billerCode,
				ServiceCategoryCode: if ($.lineOfBusiness.billerCode == "I")"D" else $.lineOfBusiness.billerCode,
				ServiceCodeDescription: $.itemCodeDescription,
				ServiceCodeLongDescription: $.itemCodeLongDescription,
				OccurrenceCode: $.outlet,				
				ServiceIdentifier: $.serviceIdentifier,
				ServiceRate: if (accountNumberSize == 16)$.serviceDiscountedRate else $.serviceFullRate,
				ServiceStatus: $.status,
				PackageContractPrice: $.packageContractPrice,
				PackageApplication: $.packageApplication,
				PackageCode: $.packageCode,
				PackageSavingsAmount: $.packageSavingsAmount,
				ServicePaidQuantity: $.servicePaidQuantity,
				ServiceFreeQuantity: $.serviceFreeQuantity,
				ServiceInstallDate: $.connectDate,
				ServiceLastChangeDate: $.serviceLastChangeDate,				
				AddressableServiceFlag: $.addressableServiceFlag,
				SoftDisconnectFlag: $.softDisconnectFlag,
				TelephoneNumber: $.telephoneDetails.telephoneNumber,
				BillCode: $.billCode,
				CircuitID: if ($.circuitId !=null or $.circuitId !="") $.circuitId  else "",
				ShortDescription: $.itemCode.description,
				LongDescription:$.itemCode.longDescription,
				CodeType: $.serviceStandard.codeType,
				(Campaign:  {
					CampaignRate: $.discount.discountedRate,
					CampaignActive: $.discount.discountActive,
					CampaignDetails : {
						CampaignDescription: $.discount.discountDetails.discountCodeDescription,
						CampaignFromDate: $.discount.discountDetails.discountBeginDate,
						CampaignToDate: $.discount.discountDetails.discountEndDate,
						CampaignCode: $.discount.discountDetails.discountCode.billerCode
						}
				}) if $.discount != null
						
			}),
		("Equipment": ((payload[2].payload.getEquipmentResponse.equipmentList default [])  map {
				SerialNumber: $.serialNumber,
				EquipmentType: $.'type',
				EquipmentDescription: $.typeDescription,
				EquipmentStatus: if(sizeOf($.components.component.equipmentOutletStatus) > 1)$.components.component.equipmentOutletStatus[0] else $.components.component.equipmentOutletStatus,
				EquipmentMake: $.make,
				EquipmentModel: $.model.billerCode,
				EquipmentOwnership: $.ownership.billerCode,
				EquipmentCode: if (accountNumberSize == 16)$.'type' else $.model.billerCode,
				EquipmentInstallDate: $.currentLocationEntryDate,
				(StandardEquipmentValues : {
						StandardCode: $.model.billerCode,
						StandardCodeDescription: $.model.description,
						StandardTypeDescription: $.subCategory,
						DigitalCapable: $.model.videoCapability,
						DVR: $.model.dvrCapability,
						HDTVCapable: $.model.highDefinitionCapability,
						HSDCapable: $.model.dataCapability,
						DigitalPhoneCapable: $.model.phoneCapability
					}) if ($.model != null),
					
				"Component": (($.components.*component default []) map {
						MACAddress: $.macAddress,
						EquipmentCategoryDescription: $.componentCategory.enterpriseCode,
						EquipmentOccurrenceNumber: $.outlet,
						EquipmentComponentCode: $.equipmentComponentCode.billerCode,
						EquipmentPort: $.equipmentPort,
						EquipmentPortType: $.portType.billerCode,
						EquipmentRatingCode: $.ratingCode,
						(($.*itemList default []) filter ($ !='') map {
						(EquipmentServiceList : {
							
							EquipmentService: {
								ServiceIdentifier: $.item.serviceIdentifier,
								EquipmentServiceCode: $.item.code.billerCode
								}
							})if ($.*itemList != null)
						})
					})
				})) if (payload[2].payload.getEquipmentResponse != null)

}

	<flow name="send-data-sequentialFlow" doc:id="89d3bf13-e102-47a7-8e10-c15da101b3ae" >

	<configuration defaultErrorHandler-ref="global-error-handler"/>

	<global-property doc:name="Global Property" doc:id="dfaec442-840f-4591-b222-701bfff6c54c" name="env" value="local" />
	
	<!-- This property's real value must be set at environment level, not here. -->
	<global-property doc:name="Global Property" doc:id="966da4dd-570c-4921-b005-e9d11b4defca" name="crypto.key" value="ThisIsADummyValue" />
	
	<!-- Properties loaded using secure-properties:config in Mule 4.x now can 
		only be addressed by adding a secure:: prefix to them. Since that is verbose 
		and could limit portability (by requiring tests to know secure vs. not secure). 
		We have both properties processors process the same files. That way non secure 
		properties can be accessed the usual way and you only have to use secure:: 
		for encrypted properties. -->
	<configuration-properties
		file="properties/${env}.api.properties" />
	<configuration-properties
		file="properties/common.api.properties" />
	<configuration-properties
		file="properties/project-app.properties" />

							   password="${secure::listener.keystore.password}"/>
			</tls:context>
		</http:listener-connection>
	</http:listener-config-->
	<error-handler name="globalErrorHandler" doc:id="5c2b1342-2977-46c1-9164-02f826bf3b19" >

</mule>

instruction: The RPA PAPI (Process API) is a MuleSoft-based integration service that bridges the gap between the **UiPath Robotic Process Automation (RPA)** platfo using DataWeave Transform, HTTP Listener with API endpoint /${api.name}/api/${api.version}/*
output:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<mule xmlns:json-logger="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/json-logger" xmlns:tracking="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/tracking" xmlns:error-handler-plugin="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/error-handler-plugin" xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core" xmlns:apikit="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/mule-apikit" xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation" xmlns:ee="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core" xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsi:schemaLocation="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/mule-apikit http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/mule-apikit/current/mule-apikit.xsd http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core/current/mule-ee.xsd 
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/error-handler-plugin http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/error-handler-plugin/current/mule-error-handler-plugin.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/tracking http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/tracking/current/mule-tracking-ee.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/json-logger http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/json-logger/current/mule-json-logger.xsd">
    <flow name="rpa-papi-main">
        <http:listener path="/${api.name}/api/${api.version}/*" config-ref="HTTPS_Listener_config">
            <http:response statusCode="#[vars.httpStatus default 200]">
                <http:headers><![CDATA[#[vars.outboundHeaders default {}]]]></http:headers>
            </http:response>
            <http:error-response statusCode="#[vars.httpStatus default 500]">
                <http:body><![CDATA[#[payload]]]></http:body>
                <http:headers><![CDATA[#[vars.outboundHeaders default {}]]]></http:headers>
            </http:error-response>
        </http:listener>
        <json-logger:logger doc:name="Log initial request payload" doc:id="de1369f3-06cc-48a2-988b-4a04ada8746e" config-ref="JSON_Logger_Config" message="Initial Request Payload" />
		<apikit:router config-ref="rpa-papi-config"/>
		<error-handler >
			<on-error-propagate enableNotifications="true" logException="true" doc:name="On Error Propagate" doc:id="6f1dd47a-2a62-4f0f-9f42-001245da6920" >
				<error-handler-plugin:on-error doc:name="Process Error" doc:id="e3ec7c5f-7387-438f-88e8-6e7bb9cc7563" config-ref="Error_Handler_Plugin_Config"/>
				<set-variable value="#[attributes.httpStatus]" doc:name="Set http status code" doc:id="e474630c-3c40-4c74-9872-fd316ca93f65" variableName="httpStatus"/>
				<json-logger:logger doc:name="Log error details" doc:id="b8e4aa7c-cdb9-42bf-9aa5-2af5c79cdf9b" config-ref="JSON_Logger_Config" message="API Kit router error details" tracePoint="EXCEPTION" priority="ERROR"/>
			</on-error-propagate>
		</error-handler>
    </flow>
	<flow name="get:\health-check:rpa-papi-config">
        <ee:transform doc:name="health-check response">
            <ee:message>
                <ee:set-payload><![CDATA[%dw 2.0
output application/json
---
{
    "success": true,
    "apiName": p('api.name'),
    "version": p('api.version'),
    "correlationId": correlationId,
    "timestamp": now(),
    "response": {
                  "Status": p('api.name') ++ " is up and running"
                }
}
 ]]></ee:set-payload>
            </ee:message>
        </ee:transform>
		<json-logger:logger doc:name="Log health check response" doc:id="a415dead-fb7a-4d93-a950-f995b9bb32a8" config-ref="JSON_Logger_Config" message="Health check response" tracePoint="END"/>
    </flow>
    <flow name="post:\addQueueItem:rpa-papi-config">
		<flow-ref doc:name="add-queue flow" doc:id="4e024df4-d5fc-407b-a0d3-6703ca45a7a3" name="add-queue"/>
    </flow>
	<flow name="post:\botProcessResult:rpa-papi-config" doc:id="e99ee4ae-2037-4094-ae17-07a3e51e43b0" >
		<flow-ref doc:name="Flow Reference" doc:id="2d50737c-d8c8-45fe-9824-8e7bb83df04d" name="bot-processFlow"/>
	</flow>
</mule>

instruction: Write a DataWeave transformation for v spcBssOnlyRecords (mapping/transformation, filtering)

import * from dw::core::Arrays
fun compareKey(obj) = (obj.Service_Code__c as String)++"|"++(obj.Service_Occurance__c as String)++"|"++(obj.Service_Quantity__c as String)++"|"++(obj.Service_Rate__c as String)
var sfBssKeys = payload.sfBssnResponse map compareKey($)
---
payload.spcBssResponse filter (not (sfBssKeys contains compareKey($)))

instruction: Write a DataWeave transformation for v mac73 response list (mapping/transformation, filtering, flattening nested data)
output:
%dw 2.0
output application/json
var billWith = (vars.Received_Service_Request.NtlSalesICOMSWorkOrderParams.Service_Request__c.Service_Location__r.Ntl_Sales_Bill_With_Account_Number__c as Number) as String
---
flatten([vars.MAC00073_Response.ICOMS.MAC00073.*INL01520 default []] ++ [vars.MAC00073_Response.ICOMS.MAC00073.*INL00783 default []]) map ((iNL01520, indexOfINL01520) ->
	{
		"billwith" : billWith,
		"occurence" : iNL01520.@SRVCOCRNC,
	   "SRVCCTGRYCODE" : if(!isEmpty(iNL01520.@SRVCCTGRYCODE)) (iNL01520.@SRVCCTGRYCODE) 
	                     else if(!isEmpty(vars.mac100Response filter ($.SERVICE_CODE == iNL01520.@FORCOCRNCSRVC) map ((item3, index3) -> item3.SRVCCTGRYCODE3)joinBy ""))
	                     	vars.mac100Response filter ($.SERVICE_CODE == iNL01520.@FORCOCRNCSRVC) map ((item3, index3) -> item3.SRVCCTGRYCODE3)joinBy ""
	                     else (vars.OLI_MRC_MAP filter ((item4, index) -> iNL01520.@FORCOCRNCSRVC == item4.Ntl_ICOMS_Service_Code__c))[0].ServiceCategoryCode
	}
)

instruction: Write a DataWeave transformation: p integration error
output:
%dw 2.0
import * from dw::core::Strings
output application/java
fun extracterrorMessage(err) =
    if (!isEmpty(err.errorMessage.error.reason)) 
        err.errorMessage.error.reason
    else if (err.errorMessage.error is Object and !isEmpty(err.errorMessage.error.message)) 
        err.errorMessage.error.message
    else if (err.errorMessage.error is Object and !isEmpty(err.errorMessage.error.error)) 
        err.errorMessage.error.error
    else if (!isEmpty(err.description)) 
        err.description
    else if (!isEmpty(err.cause.localizedMessage)) 
        err.cause.localizedMessage
    else 
        err.description

---
[{
    Name: Mule::p("api.name"),
    Error_Message__c: 
        if (error.suppressedErrors[0] != null) 
            extracterrorMessage(error.suppressedErrors[0])
        else 
            extracterrorMessage(error),
    
    Application__c: Mule::p("api.name"),
    Summary__c: Mule::p("error.summary"),
	Raw_Error_Message__c: write(error, "application/java") as String,
	Payload__c: write(vars.originalPayload, "application/json") as String,
    Error_Reason__c: 
    	if(error.suppressedErrors[0] != null)
    		error.suppressedErrors[0].errorType.asString 
    	else
    		error.errorType.asString,
    Error_Source__c: 
        if ((error.suppressedErrors.errorType.asString contains "SALESFORCE") or(error.errorType.asString contains 'SALESFORCE'))
        	 "Salesforce"
        else if ((error.suppressedErrors.errorType.asString contains "HTTP") or (error.errorType.namespace contains 'HTTP'))
        	 "NetCracker"
        else "MuleSoft",
	Relevant_Object_ID__c: vars.originalPayload.opportunityId,
	Relevant_Object_Type__c: Mule::p('salesforce.relevantObjectType'),
	Opportunity__c: vars.originalPayload.opportunityId
}]

Write a DataWeave transformation for shakarbisetty mulesoft cookbook soap to rest (mapping/transformation, XML handling)
output:
/**
 * Pattern: SOAP to REST
 * Category: Real-World Mappings
 * Difficulty: Intermediate
 * Description: Transform a SOAP XML envelope response into a clean REST JSON
 * response. A core pattern in API-led connectivity — legacy SOAP services are
 * wrapped by MuleSoft Experience/Process APIs that expose modern REST/JSON
 * interfaces to consumers.
 *
 * Input (application/xml):
 * <soap:Envelope xmlns:soap="http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/soap/envelope/"
 *   xmlns:cust="http://legacy.acme.com/customer/v1">
 *   <soap:Body>
 *     <cust:GetCustomerResponse>
 *       <cust:Customer status="active">
 *         <cust:CustomerId>C-100</cust:CustomerId>
 *         <cust:FirstName>Alice</cust:FirstName>
 *         <cust:LastName>Chen</cust:LastName>
 *         <cust:Email>alice@acme.com</cust:Email>
 *       </cust:Customer>
 *     </cust:GetCustomerResponse>
 *   </soap:Body>
 * </soap:Envelope>
 *
 * Output (application/json):
 * {
 * "customerId": "C-100",
 * "firstName": "Alice",
 * "lastName": "Chen",
 * "email": "alice@acme.com",
 * "phone": "+1-555-0142",
 * "status": "active",
 * "tier": "gold",
 * "addresses": [
 * {"type": "billing", "street": "123 Main St", "city": "San Francisco", "state": "CA", "postalCode": "94102"},
 * {"type": "shipping", "street": "456 Oak Ave", "city": "San Francisco", "state": "CA", "postalCode": "94108"}
 * ],
 * "metadata": {
 * "transactionId": "TXN-20260215-001",
 * "timestamp": "2026-02-15T14:30:00Z"
 * }
 * }
 */
%dw 2.0
output application/json
ns soap http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/soap/envelope/
ns cust http://legacy.acme.com/customer/v1
var customer = payload.soap#Envelope.soap#Body.cust#GetCustomerResponse.cust#Customer
---
{customerId: customer.cust#CustomerId, firstName: customer.cust#FirstName, lastName: customer.cust#LastName, email: customer.cust#Email, status: customer.@status}

    <http:listener-config name="munit-example-httpListenerConfig">
        <http:listener-connection host="0.0.0.0" port="8081" />
    </http:listener-config>
    <apikit:config name="munit-example-config" api="munit-example.raml" outboundHeadersMapName="outboundHeaders" httpStatusVarName="httpStatus" />
    <flow name="munit-example-main">

output:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

<mule xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http"
	xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core"
	xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation"
	xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
	xsi:schemaLocation="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd">
	<http:listener-config name="HTTP_Listener_config"
		doc:name="HTTP Listener config"
		doc:id="1c35e688-578a-49da-8271-ce96aa7aeb71">
		<http:listener-connection host="0.0.0.0"
			port="8081" />
		<http:listener-interceptors>
			<http:cors-interceptor>
				<http:origins>
					<!-- Preflight call with HTTP method OPTIONS sends three headers: - 
						Access-Control-Request-Method - Origin - Access-Control-Request-Headers -->
					<http:origin url="https://www.test-cors.org"
						accessControlMaxAge="30">
						<http:allowed-methods>
							<http:method methodName="POST" />
						</http:allowed-methods>
						<http:allowed-headers>
							<!-- If prefight call has any custom request headers in Access-Control-Request-Headers 
								header which is/are other than below, CORS will not accept -->
							<http:header headerName="authorization" />
							<http:header headerName="corr-id" />
						</http:allowed-headers>
					</http:origin>
				</http:origins>
			</http:cors-interceptor>
		</http:listener-interceptors>
	</http:listener-config>
	<flow name="mule-cors-policyFlow"
		doc:id="d2029933-640c-47ee-aa77-d4609a9fd3b6">
		<http:listener doc:name="Listener"
			doc:id="fc74939a-b5ba-4c85-95fe-1dc3ddfd9318"
			config-ref="HTTP_Listener_config" path="/cors" />
		<set-payload value="Hello..You passed thru CORS policy"
			doc:name="Set Payload" doc:id="1e6d876e-407b-4fdf-953a-31df7fff3745" />
		<logger level="INFO" doc:name="Logger"
			doc:id="e3ce3b09-c707-44b4-bb27-17b42466c9ad" message="#[payload]" />
	</flow>
</mule>

		<http:listener-connection host="0.0.0.0" port="8081" />
	</http:listener-config>
	<flow name="return-requested-w3schools-data" doc:id="c3f2b360-e71c-44f4-b539-bd3cf8844e88" >
		<http:listener doc:name="/get" doc:id="7ed9d960-605d-4bdf-b5dc-850f84ce5ee2" config-ref="HTTP_Listener_config" path="/w3schools"/>
		<ee:transform doc:name="read data to JSON output" doc:id="5a33ebf7-139b-4fbe-bab4-f89f7a396999" >
			<ee:message >
				<ee:set-payload ><![CDATA[%dw 2.0
output application/json
var requested_file = attributes.queryParams.data default ""
var file_path = "classpath://w3schools/" ++ requested_file ++  ".csv"
---
readUrl(file_path, 'application/csv')	]]></ee:set-payload>
			</ee:message>
		</ee:transform>
	</flow>
	<flow name="return-requested-field-from-airports-dataset" doc:id="11f4b04d-cf4f-4559-a37b-2eb9bda65ea4" >
		<http:listener doc:name="/get" doc:id="317c83c5-0be7-45e2-bc8a-222d4f8f72b0" config-ref="HTTP_Listener_config" path="/airports"/>
		<ee:transform doc:name="get requested field" doc:id="0a72d7ec-8abe-4443-a32b-e3b25bc260f7" >
			<ee:message >
				<ee:set-payload ><![CDATA[%dw 2.0
output application/json

var requested_field = attributes.queryParams.field default ""

var file_path = "classpath://airports/airports.json"
var data = readUrl(file_path, 'application/json')
---
data[requested_field] distinctBy $ orderBy $ // returning ordered distinct values ]]></ee:set-payload>
			</ee:message>
		</ee:transform>
	</flow>
	
	
	
	<flow name="return-requested-w3schools-data2" doc:id="4ff6ab6c-b031-4ce0-8a4c-0c1df2c6339d" >
		<http:listener doc:name="/get" doc:id="8d581d86-f2b6-49b6-966b-2c423c4fc3db" config-ref="HTTP_Listener_config" path="/w3schools2"/>
		<ee:transform doc:name="read data to JSON output" doc:id="41979ca0-8bc8-45fa-a3d3-3127bc9f00f2" >
			<ee:message >
				<ee:set-payload ><![CDATA[%dw 2.0
output application/json

import * from dw::Runtime
var requested_file = attributes.queryParams.data default ""
var file_path = "classpath://w3schools/" ++ requested_file ++  ".csv"	
---
try(() -> readUrl(file_path, 'application/json'))
orElseTry(() -> readUrl(file_path, 'application/xml'))
orElseTry(() -> readUrl(file_path, 'application/csv'))
orElse(() -> fail(requested_file ++ " was not found. Please try again!"))

]]></ee:set-payload>
			</ee:message>
		</ee:transform>
	</flow>
	<flow name="return-airport-info" doc:id="b931d130-e7b1-488a-8033-6f6cadf165a9" >

output application/json

---
data filter $.name == payload.airportName]]></ee:set-payload>
			</ee:message>
		</ee:transform>
		<logger level="INFO" doc:name="Logger" doc:id="1215db27-dd5c-4359-b68a-17285688f504" />
	</flow>
	<flow name="dataweave-lookup" doc:id="778f456f-3433-4676-99b5-001c00b7433c" >

		<ee:transform doc:name="Transform Message" doc:id="88325fca-48ea-48f1-84a1-605fba0924e3" >

output application/json
---

instruction: Write a DataWeave transformation: shakarbisetty mulesoft cookbook base64 encoding
output:
/**
 * Pattern: Base64 Encode/Decode
 * Category: Security & Encoding
 * Difficulty: Beginner
 * Description: Encode and decode binary data using Base64. Common for
 * file attachments, basic auth headers, and binary payload transport.
 *
 * Input (application/json):
 * {
 *   "username": "admin",
 *   "password": "secret123",
 *   "fileContent": "Hello, World!",
 *   "encodedData": "SGVsbG8gRnJvbSBCYXNlNjQ="
 * }
 *
 * Output (application/json):
 * {
 * "basicAuthHeader": "Authorization: Basic YWRtaW46c2VjcmV0MTIz",
 * "encodedFile": "SGVsbG8sIFdvcmxkIQ==",
 * "decodedData": "Hello, World!",
 * "roundTrip": "Hello, World!"
 * }
 */
%dw 2.0
import * from dw::core::Binaries
output application/json
---
{
  basicAuth: "Basic " ++ toBase64((payload.username ++ ":" ++ payload.password) as Binary {encoding: "UTF-8"}),
  encodedFile: toBase64(payload.fileContent as Binary {encoding: "UTF-8"}),
  decoded: fromBase64(payload.encodedData) as String {encoding: "UTF-8"},
  roundTrip: fromBase64(toBase64(payload.fileContent as Binary {encoding: "UTF-8"})) as String {encoding: "UTF-8"}
}

instruction: Write a DataWeave transformation for shakarbisetty mulesoft cookbook namespace handling (XML handling)
output:
/**
 * Pattern: Namespace Handling
 * Category: XML Handling
 * Difficulty: Advanced
 * Description: Work with XML namespaces — declare, read, transform, and strip
 * them. SOAP services, OAGIS, HL7, and enterprise XML schemas rely heavily
 * on namespaces. Understanding how DataWeave handles ns prefixes is essential
 * for B2B and enterprise integrations.
 *
 * Input (application/xml):
 * <soap:Envelope xmlns:soap="http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/soap/envelope/"
 *                xmlns:ord="http://acme.com/orders/v2">
 *   <soap:Body>
 *     <ord:GetOrderResponse>
 *       <ord:Order>
 *         <ord:OrderId>ORD-2026-1587</ord:OrderId>
 *         <ord:Customer>Acme Corporation</ord:Customer>
 *         <ord:Total currency="USD">1049.85</ord:Total>
 *       </ord:Order>
 *     </ord:GetOrderResponse>
 *   </soap:Body>
 * </soap:Envelope>
 *
 * Output (application/json):
 * {
 * "orderId": "ORD-2026-1587",
 * "customer": "Acme Corporation",
 * "total": 1049.85,
 * "currency": "USD"
 * }
 */
%dw 2.0
output application/json
ns ord http://acme.com/orders/v2
var order = payload.soap#Envelope.soap#Body.ord#GetOrderResponse.ord#Order
---
{
    orderId: order.ord#OrderId,
    customer: order.ord#Customer,
    total: order.ord#Total as Number,
    currency: order.ord#Total.@currency
}

instruction: Write a DataWeave transformation: shyamrajprasad dataweave fun zip
output:
%dw 2.0
output application/json
var myvar = {
   "list1": ["a", "b"],
   "list2": [1, 2, 3],
   "list3": ["aa", "bb"],
   "list4": [["A", "B", "C"], [11, 12], ["aA", "bB"]]
}
---
{
  "a" : [0, 1, 2, 3] zip ["a", "b", "c", "d"],
  "b" : [0, 1, 2, 3] zip ["a"],
  "c" : [0, 1, 2, 3] zip ["a", "b"],
  "d" : [0, 1, 2] zip ["a", "b", "c", "d"],
  "e" : ((myvar.list1 zip myvar.list2) zip myvar.list3) zip myvar.list4
}

var newUpdatedBssRecordCount = if (payload.upsertedBssIds != null and ((sizeOf(payload.upsertedBssIds)) > (sizeOf(payload.disconnectedBssRecords)))) ((sizeOf(payload.upsertedBssIds)) - (sizeOf(payload.disconnectedBssRecords))) else 0
var includeParams = vars.sfdc.retrieveParameters default false
---
{
	BssRefreshResponse: {
	isSuccess : true,

	disconnectedBssRecordCount : sizeOf(payload.disconnectedBssRecords) ,   
    (newUpdatedBssParamsRecordCount : if (payload.upsertedBssParamsIds != null and ((sizeOf(payload.upsertedBssParamsIds)) > (sizeOf(payload.disconnectedBssParamsRecords)))) ((sizeOf(payload.upsertedBssParamsIds)) - (sizeOf(payload.disconnectedBssParamsRecords))) else 0) if (includeParams) , 
    (disconnectedBssParamsRecordCount : sizeOf(payload.disconnectedBssParamsRecords)) if (includeParams) , 
	errorMessage : ""
	}
}

Write a DataWeave transformation: shakarbisetty mulesoft cookbook boolean handling
output:
/**
 * Pattern: Boolean Handling
 * Category: Type Coercion
 * Difficulty: Beginner
 * Description: Convert between boolean values and their string/number
 * representations. Different systems represent booleans differently —
 * true/false, "Y"/"N", 1/0, "yes"/"no", "true"/"false", "T"/"F". This
 * pattern covers all common conversions.
 *
 * Input (application/json):
 * {
 *   "isActive": true,
 *   "isVerified": false,
 *   "legacyFlag": "Y",
 *   "sapIndicator": "X",
 *   "numericBool": 1,
 *   "stringBool": "true",
 *   "yesNoField": "yes"
 * }
 *
 * Output (application/json):
 * {
 * "activeAsString": "true",
 * "activeAsYN": "Y",
 * "activeAsNumber": 1,
 * "verifiedAsYN": "N",
 * "legacyAsBool": true,
 * "sapAsBool": true,
 * "numericAsBool": true,
 * "stringAsBool": true,
 * "yesNoAsBool": true
 * }
 */
%dw 2.0
output application/json
fun toBoolean(val) = val match {case is Boolean -> val case "Y" -> true case "N" -> false case "X" -> true case "" -> false case "yes" -> true case "no" -> false case is Number -> val != 0 case is String -> val as Boolean}
---
{isActive: toBoolean(payload.isActive), isVerified: toBoolean(payload.isVerified), legacyFlag: toBoolean(payload.legacyFlag), sapIndicator: toBoolean(payload.sapIndicator), numericBool: toBoolean(payload.numericBool), stringBool: toBoolean(payload.stringBool), yesNoField: toBoolean(payload.yesNoField)}

output application/java

{

			<when expression="#[sizeOf(payload) > 0]" >

				<choice doc:name="Check access token not null" doc:id="d700f4e5-93eb-4690-b7a1-1b884a1a3472" >
					<when expression="#[vars.token.access_token != null]" >
						<http:request method="POST" doc:name="Request to Pega to process construct contact object" doc:id="d6d38046-11b5-48b4-b96b-54691e7a6862" config-ref="HTTP_Request_configuration-pegacloud" path="${pega.update_resource}" responseTimeout="${http.responsetimeout}" >
							<reconnect frequency="30000" count="3" />
							<http:headers ><![CDATA[#[output application/java
---
{
	"Authorization" : "Bearer " ++ vars.token.access_token 
}]]]></http:headers>
						</http:request>
						<ee:transform doc:name="Success Response Payload" doc:id="7f39f769-4917-489b-a165-fe2eb4970ed7">
							<ee:message>
								<ee:set-payload resource="dwl/updateResponseMapping.dwl" />
							</ee:message>
						</ee:transform>
						<json-logger:logger doc:name="Log success response" doc:id="467f5dbd-433e-40c7-863c-8ed623b6984b" config-ref="JSON_Logger_Config" message="Response for insert/update schedule info into pega" tracePoint="END" />
					</when>
					<otherwise >
						<ee:transform doc:name="Error details" doc:id="900e9d58-62ca-4c0f-b34a-b3a8577f1272" >
							<ee:message >
								<ee:set-payload ><![CDATA[%dw 2.0
output application/json
---
{
    "success": false,
    "apiName": p('api.name'),
    "version": p('api.version'),
    "correlationId": correlationId,
    "timestamp": now(),
    "errorDetails":    [
        {
            "code": p('error.badrequestcode'), 
            "reason": p('error.tokenreason'),
            "message":p('error.tokenmessage'), 
        }
    ]  
    
}
]]></ee:set-payload>
							</ee:message>
							<ee:variables >
								<ee:set-variable variableName="httpStatus" ><![CDATA[%dw 2.0
output application/java
---
p('error.badrequestcode')]]></ee:set-variable>
							</ee:variables>
						</ee:transform>
						<json-logger:logger doc:name="Log invalid token message" doc:id="a685896c-0b7c-4f8e-a9cc-c371fe30ca3d" config-ref="JSON_Logger_Config" message="Invalid token error message" tracePoint="END" priority="ERROR" />
					</otherwise>
				</choice>
			</when>
			<otherwise >
				<json-logger:logger doc:name="Log the empty payload" doc:id="a7167c4d-b876-4898-8b93-1b382e5834a1" config-ref="JSON_Logger_Config" message="Consumed empty payload from XDG - Payload should not be empty" tracePoint="EXCEPTION" priority="ERROR" />
			</otherwise>
		</choice>
		<error-handler ref="global_error_handler" />
	</flow>
</mule>

    "createdDate": now(),
    "objectType": "Service_Request__c"
}

instruction: Write a DataWeave transformation for shakarbisetty mulesoft cookbook json to csv (mapping/transformation, CSV processing)
output:
/**
 * Pattern: JSON to CSV
 * Category: CSV Operations
 * Difficulty: Beginner
 * Description: Convert a JSON array of objects to CSV format with headers.
 * Use when exporting data for spreadsheet consumption, generating bulk
 * upload files, creating reports, or feeding data to legacy systems
 * that only accept CSV input.
 *
 * Input (application/json):
 * [
 *   {
 *     "orderId": "ORD-1001",
 *     "customer": "Acme Corp",
 *     "product": "Laptop",
 *     "quantity": 5,
 *     "unitPrice": 1299.99
 *   },
 *   {
 *     "orderId": "ORD-1002",
 *     "customer": "Globex Inc",
 *     "product": "Monitor",
 *     "quantity": 10,
 *     "unitPrice": 599.99
 *   },
 *   {
 *     "orderId": "ORD-1003",
 *     "customer": "Acme Corp",
 *     "product": "Keyboard",
 *     "quantity": 20,
 *     "unitPrice": 149.99
 *   }
 * ]
 *
 * Output (application/csv):
 * orderId,customer,product,quantity,unitPrice,total,orderDate
 * ORD-1001,Acme Corp,Laptop,5,1299.99,6499.95,2026-02-10
 * ORD-1002,Globex Inc,Monitor,10,599.99,5999.90,2026-02-11
 * ORD-1003,Acme Corp,Keyboard,20,149.99,2999.80,2026-02-12
 */
%dw 2.0
output application/csv header=true
---
payload map (order) -> ({
    orderId: order.orderId,
    customer: order.customer,
    product: order.product,
    quantity: order.quantity,
    unitPrice: order.unitPrice,
    total: order.quantity * order.unitPrice
})

Write a DataWeave transformation for shakarbisetty mulesoft cookbook csv to json (mapping/transformation, CSV processing)
output:
/**
 * Pattern: CSV to JSON
 * Category: CSV Operations
 * Difficulty: Beginner
 * Description: Parse a CSV payload into a JSON array of objects, where the
 * first row provides the field names. Common when processing file-based
 * integrations, bulk imports, and data feeds from legacy systems that
 * export data as CSV/TSV files.
 *
 * Input (application/csv):
 * employeeId,firstName,lastName,department,salary,startDate
 * EMP001,Alice,Chen,Engineering,95000,2024-01-15
 * EMP002,Bob,Martinez,Marketing,72000,2025-06-20
 * EMP003,Carol,Nguyen,Engineering,110000,2023-03-10
 * EMP004,David,Kim,Sales,68000,2025-11-01
 *
 * Output (application/json):
 * [
 * {"employeeId": "EMP001", "firstName": "Alice", "lastName": "Chen", "department": "Engineering", "salary": 95000, "startDate": "2024-01-15"},
 * {"employeeId": "EMP002", "firstName": "Bob", "lastName": "Martinez", "department": "Marketing", "salary": 72000, "startDate": "2025-06-20"},
 * {"employeeId": "EMP003", "firstName": "Carol", "lastName": "Nguyen", "department": "Engineering", "salary": 110000, "startDate": "2023-03-10"},
 * {"employeeId": "EMP004", "firstName": "David", "lastName": "Kim", "department": "Sales", "salary": 68000, "startDate": "2025-11-01"}
 * ]
 */
%dw 2.0
output application/json
---
payload map (row) -> ({employeeId: row.employeeId, firstName: row.firstName, lastName: row.lastName, department: row.department, salary: row.salary as Number, startDate: row.startDate})

instruction: Write a DataWeave transformation for service requests groupby (mapping/transformation, aggregation, flattening nested data)
output:
%dw 2.0
output application/json

---

if(sizeOf(vars.serviceCategoryDetailsVar)>0)
   (
     flatten(vars.serviceCategoryDetailsVar map (  (item2, index2) -> vars.serviceRequestDetailsVar map ( (item1, index1) ->  ( if(  (item2.Service_Code__c matches(item1.Service_Code__c) ) 
and    (item2.Combined_Service_Code_Contains__c splitBy  "," reduce ((reduceitem, acc= true) -> acc and (item1.Offer__r.Combined_Service_Code__c splitBy  "," contains reduceitem  )
 )
 )
 )
 
 ( {
     "Id": item1.Service_Request__c ,
     "serviceRequestNumber": item1.Service_Request__r.Service_Request_ID__c,
     "serviceLocationId": item1.Service_Request__r.Service_Location__c,
     "serviceRequestStage": item1.Service_Request__r.Service_Request_Stage__c ,
     "region": item1.Service_Request__r.Spectrum_Region__c,
      "requestingRegion": item1.Service_Request__r.Requesting_Region__c,
     "businessUnit": item1.Service_Request__r.Billing_Account__r.ROE_Sales_Channel__c,
     "productFamily": item1.Service_Request__r.Product_Family__c,
     "serviceCategory": item2.Service_Category__c,
     "jurisdiction": item2.Jurisdiction__c,
    "orderType": item1.Service_Request__r.Service_Request_Order_Type__c,
    "changeReason": item1.Service_Request__r.Change_Reason__c ,
    "constructionJob": item1.Service_Request__r.Construction_Job__c ,
    "typeII": if(item2.Type_II__c == "true")("Y")else("N")
   
 } )

 else({
     "Id": item1.Service_Request__c ,
     "serviceRequestNumber": item1.Service_Request__r.Service_Request_ID__c,
     "serviceLocationId": item1.Service_Request__r.Service_Location__c,
     "serviceRequestStage": item1.Service_Request__r.Service_Request_Stage__c ,
     "region": item1.Service_Request__r.Spectrum_Region__c,
     "requestingRegion": item1.Service_Request__r.Requesting_Region__c,
     "businessUnit": item1.Service_Request__r.Billing_Account__r.ROE_Sales_Channel__c,
     "productFamily": item1.Service_Request__r.Product_Family__c,
     "serviceCategory": null,
     "jurisdiction": null,
    "orderType": item1.Service_Request__r.Service_Request_Order_Type__c,
    "changeReason": item1.Service_Request__r.Change_Reason__c ,
    "constructionJob": item1.Service_Request__r.Construction_Job__c ,
    "typeII": null 
    
 }) )

 
  )))  
   )   
   else 
   (flatten((vars.serviceRequestDetailsVar groupBy (item) -> item.Service_Request__c) pluck $) ) distinctBy ((item, index) -> item.Service_Request__c ) map ((item, index) -> {
     "Id": item.Service_Request__c ,
     "serviceRequestNumber": item.Service_Request__r.Service_Request_ID__c,
     "serviceLocationId": item.Service_Request__r.Service_Location__c,
     "serviceRequestStage": item.Service_Request__r.Service_Request_Stage__c ,
     "region": item.Service_Request__r.Spectrum_Region__c,
     "requestingRegion": item.Service_Request__r.Requesting_Region__c,
     "businessUnit": item.Service_Request__r.Billing_Account__r.ROE_Sales_Channel__c,
     "productFamily": item.Service_Request__r.Product_Family__c,
     "serviceCategory": null,
     "jurisdiction": null,
     "orderType": item.Service_Request__r.Service_Request_Order_Type__c,
     "changeReason": item.Service_Request__r.Change_Reason__c ,
     "constructionJob": item.Service_Request__r.Construction_Job__c ,
     "typeII": null 
    
       
   })

instruction: Write a DataWeave transformation: shyamrajprasad dataweave fun yearWeekToDate

%dw 2.0
output application/json
import * from dw::core::Dates
import * from dw::core::Periods
var yearWeekString = "201601"
fun beginningOfWeekYear(year: Number, week: Number)=
    atBeginningOfWeek(atBeginningOfWeek(date({ year: year, month: 1, day: 1})) +  days(week * 7 ))
---
{
    "201601": beginningOfWeekYear(yearWeekString[0 to 3] as Number, yearWeekString[4 to -1] as Number),
    "202041": beginningOfWeekYear(2020, 41)
}

instruction: Write a DataWeave transformation for shakarbisetty mulesoft cookbook ai prompt builder (mapping/transformation)
output:
/**
 * Pattern: AI Prompt Builder for Inference Connector
 * Category: AI Integration
 * Difficulty: Intermediate
 * Description: Build structured prompts from enterprise data for MuleSoft's
 * Inference Connector. Transforms raw business objects into well-formatted
 * LLM prompt payloads with system instructions, context injection, and
 * structured output requirements.
 *
 * Input (application/json):
 * {
 *   "customer": {
 *     "name": "Acme Corp",
 *     "tier": "Enterprise"
 *   },
 *   "ticketHistory": [
 *     {
 *       "id": "TK-101",
 *       "subject": "API timeout",
 *       "status": "open",
 *       "priority": "high"
 *     },
 *     {
 *       "id": "TK-098",
 *       "subject": "OAuth refresh failing",
 *       "status": "open",
 *       "priority": "medium"
 *     },
 *     {
 *       "id": "TK-095",
 *       "subject": "Batch stuck at 80 pct",
 *       "status": "open",
 *       "priority": "low"
 *     }
 *   ],
 *   "model": "gpt-4o",
 *   "maxTokens": 500
 * }
 *
 * Output (application/json):
 * {
 * "model": "gpt-4o",
 * "max_tokens": 500,
 * "messages": [
 * {"role": "system", "content": "You are an enterprise support analyst..."},
 * {"role": "user", "content": "Analyze the following tickets for Acme Corp (Enterprise tier)..."}
 * ],
 * "response_format": {"type": "json_object"}
 * }
 */
%dw 2.0
output application/json
var systemPrompt = "You are an enterprise support analyst."
var lines = payload.ticketHistory map (t) -> "- [$(t.priority upper)] $(t.id): $(t.subject)"
var userPrompt = "Analyze tickets for $(payload.customer.name):\n" ++ (lines joinBy "\n")
---
{
  model: payload.model,
  max_tokens: payload.maxTokens,
  messages: [
    {role: "system", content: systemPrompt},
    {role: "user", content: userPrompt}
  ]
}

	"A+"
else if(p>=80 and p<90)
	"A"

instruction: Write a DataWeave transformation for shakarbisetty mulesoft cookbook streaming reduce (aggregation)
output:
/**
 * Pattern: Streaming with Reduce
 * Category: Performance & Optimization
 * Difficulty: Advanced
 * Description: Use reduce to process large datasets in a single pass
 * without buffering. Compute aggregations (sum, count, min, max, running
 * totals) while streaming through records.
 *
 * Input (application/json):
 * [
 *   {
 *     "product": "Widget",
 *     "amount": 500,
 *     "units": 20
 *   },
 *   {
 *     "product": "Gadget",
 *     "amount": 1200,
 *     "units": 15
 *   },
 *   {
 *     "product": "Widget",
 *     "amount": 800,
 *     "units": 30
 *   },
 *   {
 *     "product": "Gadget",
 *     "amount": 600,
 *     "units": 10
 *   },
 *   {
 *     "product": "Gizmo",
 *     "amount": 950,
 *     "units": 25
 *   }
 * ]
 *
 * Output (application/json):
 * {
 * "totalRevenue": 8650,
 * "totalUnits": 220,
 * "recordCount": 5,
 * "avgOrderValue": 1730,
 * "maxOrder": { "amount": 3100, "product": "Widget", "region": "East" },
 * "revenueByProduct": { "Widget": 5400, "Gadget": 3250 },
 * "revenueByRegion": { "West": 3250, "East": 5400 }
 * }
 */
%dw 2.0
output application/json
var stats = payload reduce (item, acc = {totalRevenue: 0, totalUnits: 0, count: 0, maxAmount: 0, maxOrder: {}, byProduct: {}}) -> ({
  totalRevenue: acc.totalRevenue + item.amount,
  totalUnits: acc.totalUnits + item.units,
  count: acc.count + 1,
  maxAmount: if (item.amount > acc.maxAmount) item.amount else acc.maxAmount,
  maxOrder: if (item.amount > acc.maxAmount) {amount: item.amount, product: item.product} else acc.maxOrder,
  byProduct: acc.byProduct ++ {(item.product): (acc.byProduct[item.product] default 0) + item.amount}})
---
{totalRevenue: stats.totalRevenue, totalUnits: stats.totalUnits, avgOrderValue: round(stats.totalRevenue / stats.count), maxOrder: stats.maxOrder, revenueByProduct: stats.byProduct}

instruction: Write a DataWeave transformation for shakarbisetty mulesoft cookbook conditional error (mapping/transformation, filtering, XML handling)
output:
/**
 * Pattern: Conditional Error Handling
 * Category: Error Handling
 * Difficulty: Intermediate
 * Description: Validate input data and conditionally handle errors or set
 * defaults based on business rules. Use for field-level validation, business
 * rule enforcement, and graceful degradation when input data doesn't meet
 * expected criteria.
 *
 * Input (application/json):
 * {
 *   "orders": [
 *     {
 *       "orderId": "ORD-001",
 *       "customer": "Acme Corp",
 *       "amount": 5000
 *     },
 *     {
 *       "orderId": "ORD-002",
 *       "customer": "",
 *       "amount": -50
 *     },
 *     {
 *       "orderId": "ORD-003",
 *       "customer": "Bolt Inc",
 *       "amount": 150
 *     },
 *     {
 *       "orderId": "ORD-004",
 *       "customer": "Delta LLC",
 *       "amount": 0
 *     }
 *   ]
 * }
 *
 * Output (application/json):
 * {
 * "validOrders": [
 * {"orderId": "ORD-001", "customer": "Acme Corp", "amount": 5000, "currency": "USD", "priority": "HIGH", "valid": true},
 * {"orderId": "ORD-003", "customer": "Globex Inc", "amount": 150, "currency": "EUR", "priority": "NORMAL", "valid": true}
 * ],
 * "invalidOrders": [
 * {"orderId": "ORD-002", "errors": ["Customer name is required", "Amount must be positive", "Invalid currency: INVALID"]},
 * {"orderId": "ORD-004", "errors": ["Amount must be positive"]}
 * ]
 * }
 */
%dw 2.0
output application/json
fun validateOrder(order) =
  if (!(order.customer is String) or order.customer == "") "Missing customer"
  else if (order.amount default 0 <= 0) "Amount must be greater than 0"
  else null
var checked = payload.orders map (o) -> o ++ {error: validateOrder(o)}
---
{
  validOrders: checked filter ($.error == null) map ($ - "error"),
  invalidOrders: checked filter ($.error != null)
}

instruction: Write a DataWeave transformation for p MAC00073RequestCheckMACErrors (XML handling)
Write a DataWeave transformation for IOMResponsexmltojson (XML handling)
output:
%dw 2.0
import withMaxSize from dw::core::Strings
output application/java
var statusDetail = (vars.TAPResponse.updateStatus.newStatus.*errorInformationArray.errorMessage joinBy "") default null
var newStatus = vars.TAPResponse.updateStatus.newStatus
---
{
	IOMResponse : [
		{	 	
			Status__c: newStatus.*status[0],
			Status_Details__c: if(statusDetail != null and (sizeOf (statusDetail) < 10000)) statusDetail  else statusDetail withMaxSize 10000 default'' ,
			OrderVersion__c:  if (newStatus.sourceClientId.orderVersion != "") newStatus.sourceClientId.orderVersion else "",
				Service_request__c :  if (newStatus.equipmentSwapId !=null) (newStatus.equipmentSwapId) 
					else if ( newStatus.sourceClientId.orderNumber != null and newStatus.sourceClientId.orderNumber != "" and (sizeOf (newStatus.sourceClientId.orderNumber)) > 18)  (newStatus.sourceClientId.orderNumber withMaxSize 18)
					 else newStatus.sourceClientId.orderNumber,
			ServiceRequestVersion__c :  if (newStatus.sourceClientId != null and newStatus.sourceClientId.orderVersion != "") newStatus.sourceClientId.orderVersion else "",
			Address_ID__c :  if (newStatus.addressID != "") newStatus.addressID else "",
			Message_Type__c : "StatusUpdate",
			BillingAccountNumber__c: newStatus.BillingAccountNumber,
			OrderNumber__c :  if (newStatus.serviceRequestNumber != "") newStatus.serviceRequestNumber else "",
			Validation_Status__c :  if (newStatus.validationStatus != "") newStatus.validationStatus else "",
			Supplement_Type_Text__c :  if (newStatus.sourceClientId.orderVersionReason  == "1" ) "1 – cancel an existing order"  else if (newStatus.sourceClientId.orderVersionReason  == "2") "2 - reschedule an existing order" else if (newStatus.sourceClientId.orderVersionReason == "3") "3 - alter the content of an existing order (can include a reschedule)" else if (newStatus.sourceClientId.orderVersionReason == "8") "8 - Switch only changes" else ""
		}
			
	]
	}

})

fun getMatchedEpr(srId) = ((vars.srEprRecords.Engineering__r[0]) filter $.Service_Request__c == srId).Id[0]
---
vars.modifiedPayload.sfdcServiceRequestId[0] map ((item, index) -> {
	(NEOS__c: vars.modifiedPayload[0].managedByPega)if(vars.modifiedPayload.managedByPega != null),
	(NEOS_Product_Order__c: vars.modifiedPayload[0].pegaProductOrder) if(vars.modifiedPayload.pegaProductOrder != null),
	(Service_Request__c: item)if(vars.modifiedPayload[0].sfdcServiceRequestId != null and (!(isEmpty(vars.validSrData)) or !(isEmpty(vars.srEprRecords)))),
	(SR_External_Id__c: item)if(vars.modifiedPayload[0].sfdcServiceRequestId != null),
	(Engineering__c: if(vars.kafkaPayload.sfdcServiceRequestId != null) vars.modifiedPayload[0].sfdcEngineeringId[0] else getMatchedEpr(item)),
	(Pega_Fulfilment_Case_Number__c: vars.modifiedPayload[0].pegaFulfilmentCaseNum)if(vars.modifiedPayload.pegaFulfilmentCaseNum != null),
	(Opportunity__c: vars.modifiedPayload[0].sfdcOpportunityId)if(vars.modifiedPayload.sfdcOpportunityId != null),
	(Pega_Case_Created_Date__c: vars.modifiedPayload[0].pegaCaseCreatedDate as DateTime)if(vars.modifiedPayload.pegaCaseCreatedDate != null),
	// Banner message
	(Pega_Banner_Message__c: (vars.modifiedPayload[0].bannerMsg)) if(vars.modifiedPayload[0].bannerMsg != null),
	(Pega_EPR_Tasks__c: if ( sizeOf(vars.modifiedPayload[0].taskList ) > 0 ) (vars.modifiedPayload[0].taskList joinBy  ";") else " ") if(vars.modifiedPayload[0].taskList != null),
	(Pega_Task_URL__c: (vars.modifiedPayload[0].pegaCaseURL)) if(vars.modifiedPayload[0].pegaCaseURL != null),
	(Pega_Short_Banner_Message__c: (vars.modifiedPayload[0].shortBannerMsg)) if(vars.modifiedPayload[0].shortBannerMsg != null),
})

instruction: Write a DataWeave transformation: shakarbisetty mulesoft cookbook url parse build
output:
/**
 * Pattern: URL Parsing and Component Extraction
 * Category: Utility Modules
 * Difficulty: Beginner
 * Description: Use dw::core::URL to decompose URLs into components and
 * build URLs from parts. Essential for dynamic API endpoint construction,
 * query parameter manipulation, redirect URL validation, and OAuth
 * callback handling.
 *
 * Input (application/json):
 * {
 *   "url": "https://api.example.com:8443/v2/orders?status=active&limit=50",
 *   "newParams": {
 *     "page": "3",
 *     "sort": "date_desc"
 *   }
 * }
 *
 * Output (application/json):
 * {
 * "parsed": {
 * "protocol": "https",
 * "host": "api.example.com",
 * "port": 8443,
 * "path": "/v2/orders",
 * "queryParams": {"status": "active", "limit": "50", "offset": "100"},
 * "fragment": "section2"
 * },
 * "rebuilt": "https://api.example.com:8443/v2/orders?page=3&sort=date_desc",
 * "baseUrl": "https://api.example.com:8443/v2/orders"
 * }
 */
%dw 2.0
import parseURI, encodeURIComponent from dw::core::URL
output application/json
var parsed = parseURI(payload.url)
var queryParts = payload.newParams pluck (v, k) -> "$(encodeURIComponent(k as String))=$(encodeURIComponent(v))"
var newQuery = queryParts joinBy "&"
var baseUrl = (parsed.scheme default "https") ++ "://" ++ (parsed.host default "") ++ (if (parsed.port != null) (":" ++ (parsed.port as String)) else "") ++ (parsed.path default "")
---
({parsed: ({protocol: parsed.scheme, host: parsed.host, port: if (parsed.port != null) parsed.port else null, path: parsed.path}), rebuilt: baseUrl ++ "?" ++ newQuery, baseUrl: baseUrl})

http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/json-logger http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/json-logger/current/mule-json-logger.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/error-handler-plugin http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/error-handler-plugin/current/mule-error-handler-plugin.xsd">
    <flow name="pega-prism-papi-main">

				<json-logger:logger doc:name="Log APIkit error response" doc:id="dee24c8a-e25d-4339-870c-b0ff72019ae3" config-ref="JSON_Logger_Config" message="Error response from APIkit router" tracePoint="EXCEPTION" priority="ERROR" />
			</on-error-propagate>

instruction: Write a DataWeave transformation: CustomFunctions
output:
%dw 2.0
import * from dw::core::Strings

fun determineServiceQuantity(servicePaidQuantity,serviceFreeQuantity) = 	if(servicePaidQuantity == null or servicePaidQuantity == '') 0 else servicePaidQuantity as Number
	+	if(serviceFreeQuantity == null or serviceFreeQuantity == '') 0 else serviceFreeQuantity as Number


fun determineTotalMRR(serviceDiscountedRate, servicePaidQuantity, status, billingSystemType) = if(serviceDiscountedRate != null and servicePaidQuantity != null) (
    if(billingSystemType == 'ICOMS8' or billingSystemType == 'ICOMS') serviceDiscountedRate
    else if(status != null and status != 'I')serviceDiscountedRate
    else 0 )
	else 0
	
	
fun determineNationalSales(billingSystemType,serviceLocationDivisionId) = if(billingSystemType == 'ICOMS8' or billingSystemType == 'ICOMS')(
	if(serviceLocationDivisionId == Mule::p('app.NationalSalesDivision')) true else false
) else false


fun determineEffectiveServiceRate(billingSystemType, serviceDiscountedRate, servicePaidQuantity, status) = if(billingSystemType == 'ICOMS8' or billingSystemType == 'ICOMS') round((serviceDiscountedRate/servicePaidQuantity)*100)/100

else if(status != 'I') (
    if( serviceDiscountedRate !=  0 and servicePaidQuantity > 0 ) round((serviceDiscountedRate/servicePaidQuantity)*100)/100 else 0) else 0
    
    
fun convertServiceStatus(billingSystemType, status) = if(billingSystemType == 'ICOMS8' or billingSystemType == 'ICOMS') (
    if(status == 'A') 'Active' 
    else if(status == 'I') 'Pending Install'
    else if(status == 'D') 'Disconnected'
    else if(status == 'N') 'Never connected'
    else 'UNKNOWN ICOMS STATUS:' ++ status
)

else if(billingSystemType == 'ACP') (
    if(status == 'A') 'Active' 
    else if(status == 'D') 'Disconnected'
    else if(status == 'I') 'Install Pending'
    else 'UNKNOWN ACP STATUS:' ++ status
) else 'UNKNOWN'



fun convertYesandNoToBoolean(addressableServiceFlag) = if(addressableServiceFlag == 'Y' or addressableServiceFlag == 'YES' or addressableServiceFlag == 'y' or addressableServiceFlag == 'yes') true else false

instruction: The **SMB National ICOMS Integration Application** automates the synchronization of billing account provisioning between **Salesforce CRM** and **Char using DataWeave Transform, Error Handling, HTTP Listener with API endpoint /${api.name}/api/${api.version}/console/*
output:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<mule xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core" xmlns:apikit="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/mule-apikit" xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation" xmlns:ee="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core" xmlns:error-handler-plugin="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/error-handler-plugin" xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http" xmlns:json-logger="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/json-logger" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsi:schemaLocation="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/mule-apikit http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/mule-apikit/current/mule-apikit.xsd http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core/current/mule-ee.xsd  http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/json-logger http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/json-logger/current/mule-json-logger.xsd http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/error-handler-plugin http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/error-handler-plugin/current/mule-error-handler-plugin.xsd">
    <flow name="smb-national-icoms-integration-application-main">
        <http:listener config-ref="HTTPS_Listener_config" path="${api.name}/api/${api.version}/*">
            <http:response statusCode="#[vars.httpStatus default 200]">
                <http:headers><![CDATA[#[vars.outboundHeaders default {}]]]></http:headers>
            </http:response>
            <http:error-response statusCode="#[vars.httpStatus default 500]">
                <http:body><![CDATA[#[payload]]]></http:body>
                <http:headers><![CDATA[#[vars.outboundHeaders default {}]]]></http:headers>
            </http:error-response>
        </http:listener>

        <apikit:router config-ref="smb-national-icoms-integration-application-config" />
        <error-handler>

                <set-variable variableName="httpStatus" value="#[attributes.httpStatus]" doc:name="Set HTTP Status Code" />

            </on-error-propagate>

    </flow>
    <flow name="smb-national-icoms-integration-application-console">

            <http:response statusCode="#[vars.httpStatus default 200]">

})

instruction: Write a DataWeave transformation for shakarbisetty mulesoft cookbook split join (XML handling, CSV processing)
output:
/**
 * Pattern: Split and Join
 * Category: String Operations
 * Difficulty: Beginner
 * Description: Split a string into an array by a delimiter, and join an array
 * back into a string. Fundamental for parsing CSV-like values, breaking apart
 * compound identifiers, building comma-separated lists, and reformatting
 * delimited data between systems.
 *
 * Input (application/json):
 * {
 *   "fullName": "Alice Marie Chen",
 *   "tags": "mulesoft,dataweave,integration,api",
 *   "filePath": "/opt/mule/apps/customer-api/src/main/mule/customer-flow.xml"
 * }
 *
 * Output (application/json):
 * {
 * "firstName": "Alice",
 * "middleName": "Marie",
 * "lastName": "Chen",
 * "tagList": ["mulesoft", "dataweave", "integration", "api"],
 * "tagDisplay": "mulesoft | dataweave | integration | api",
 * "fileName": "customer-flow.xml",
 * "fileExtension": "xml"
 * }
 */
%dw 2.0
output application/json
var nameParts = payload.fullName splitBy " "
var tags = payload.tags splitBy ","
var pathParts = payload.filePath splitBy "/"
var fileName = pathParts[-1]
---
{firstName: nameParts[0], middleName: nameParts[1], lastName: nameParts[-1], tagList: tags, tagDisplay: tags joinBy " | ", fileName: fileName, fileExtension: (fileName splitBy ".")[-1]}

instruction: Write a DataWeave transformation for shakarbisetty mulesoft cookbook fixed width generate (mapping/transformation)
output:
/**
 * Pattern: Fixed-Width Generate
 * Category: Flat File & Fixed-Width
 * Difficulty: Intermediate
 * Description: Generate fixed-width (positional) output from JSON data.
 * Pad fields to exact widths with proper alignment (left-align strings,
 * right-align numbers, zero-pad numeric codes).
 *
 * Input (application/json):
 * [
 *   {
 *     "empId": "E001",
 *     "name": "Alice Martin",
 *     "dept": "Engineering",
 *     "salary": 85000.5,
 *     "startDate": "2022-01-15"
 *   },
 *   {
 *     "empId": "E002",
 *     "name": "Bob Chen",
 *     "dept": "Sales",
 *     "salary": 72000.0,
 *     "startDate": "2023-06-01"
 *   },
 *   {
 *     "empId": "E003",
 *     "name": "Carol Davis",
 *     "dept": "Finance",
 *     "salary": 91500.75,
 *     "startDate": "2021-09-20"
 *   }
 * ]
 *
 * Output (text/plain):
 * 12345John Doe            Engineering     000095000.002020-01-15
 * 67890Jane Smith           Marketing       000082000.002021-06-01
 * 11111Bob Johnson          Sales           000078500.002019-11-20
 * 
 * Field layout:
 * EmpID:     5 chars, left-aligned
 * Name:      20 chars, left-aligned
 * Dept:      16 chars, left-aligned
 * Salary:    12 chars, right-aligned zero-padded with 2 decimals
 * StartDate: 10 chars
 */
%dw 2.0
output text/plain
fun lpad(s: String, len: Number, ch: String): String =
    if (sizeOf(s) >= len) s[0 to (len - 1)] else (ch * (len - sizeOf(s))) ++ s
fun rpad(s: String, len: Number, ch: String): String =
    if (sizeOf(s) >= len) s[0 to (len - 1)] else s ++ (ch * (len - sizeOf(s)))
fun formatAmount(n: Number, len: Number): String = lpad(n as String {format: "0.00"}, len, "0")
---
(payload map (emp) -> rpad(emp.empId, 5, " ") ++ rpad(emp.name, 20, " ") ++ rpad(emp.dept, 16, " ") ++ formatAmount(emp.salary, 12) ++ emp.startDate) joinBy "\n"
